messages.content:
You are comparing two analyst responses to determine which better demonstrates knowledge of a specific finding from a longitudinal dataset.

Question asked: Are any upstream dependencies showing degraded performance?

Finding to evaluate: geo-lookup latency degraded progressively over multiple reporting periods with p99 values increasing from baseline through each successive day rather than appearing as a single sudden spike

Response A:


Response B:
**Yes – several upstream services are showing clear signs of degradation.**  

| Dependency | Symptom | Log entry |
|------------|---------|-----------|
| **IdP API servers** | CPU usage crossed the “SEVERE” threshold (57 % on idp‑api‑01 and 53 % on idp‑api‑02) and later rose to 66 %/61 % on 2026‑01‑06. Connection‑pool exhaustion also appeared (2 exhausted connections on idp‑api‑01, 1 on idp‑api‑02). | [cascading_failure_01_dx_006] – alerts “SEVERE idp‑api‑01: CPU 57%” / “SEVERE idp‑api‑02: CPU 53%” and pool‑idp‑api‑01/02 exhaustion counts. |
| **Audit DB** | Disk‑usage warning (75 % disk) and rising IOPS, plus a “WARNING audit‑db‑01: Disk 75%”. | [cascading_failure_01_dx_006] – alerts. |
| **LDAP / Directory Sync** | “SEVERE LDAP bind timeout on directory sync” caused a run to fail on the first attempt. | [cascading_failure_01_dx_003] – alerts. |
| **SAML / OIDC health** | Multiple SAML assertion errors (12 – 28 per day) and OIDC discovery/JWKS fetch errors. | [cascading_failure_01_dx_005] – alerts “[ERROR] idp_health: 28 SAML assertion errors”, “[WARNING] idp_health: 6 OIDC discovery errors”, “[WARNING] idp_health: 2 JWKS fetch errors”. |
| **Audit pipeline ingest** | Ingest lag p95 spiked (6.2 s) and drop‑rate warnings, prompting back‑pressure tuning. | [cascading_failure_01_dx_008] – alerts “[WARNING] audit pipeline ingest lag p95: 6.2 sec” and “[WARNING] audit pipeline drop rate approaching threshold”. |
| **CDN / Caching** | Hit‑rate collapsed to 0 % (no cache hits, no bandwidth) on 2026‑01‑05, indicating a CDN outage or mis‑configuration. | [cascading_failure_01_dx_004] – “Hit rate: 0% | Bandwidth: 0 Gbps | Origin requests: 0”. |
| **Connection‑pool health** | Exhaustion flags appeared on the primary IdP pools (2 exhausted on pool‑idp‑api‑01, 1 on pool‑idp‑api‑02). | [cascading_failure_01_dx_006] – pool

Which response better demonstrates awareness of this finding? The response may use different terminology. Focus on whether the core insight is present, not exact wording.

Respond with ONLY one word: A, B, or TIE
/no_think
